what_it_does: Implements SQLite schema initialization, additive migrations, and data-model helpers for a recollection/memory substrate: it evolves the exchanges and tool_calls tables, creates per-lens sqlite-vec indexes, defines registry tables for unit types and relation types, and provides lineage assertions, unit/candidate writers, deterministic link insertion, and legacy migration utilities.
observations: Uses better-sqlite3 and loads the sqlite-vec extension; enables WAL mode.
migrateSchema adds missing columns to exchanges one at a time via ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN checks.
Introduces a per-lens vec0 table mechanism (ensureLensIndex) with dynamic double-quoted names like vec_<space> and dtype-specific binding for int8 versus float.
Includes active-axis helpers (getActiveAxis / ensureActiveAxisIndex) that read the current lens descriptor from embeddings.js.
Quantizes float vectors to int8 with Math.round to avoid truncation bias from noisy served values.
Creates additive recollection tables: unit_type, atoms, units, relation_type, links, fingerprints, verdicts, candidates.
Implements registry-is-truth lookups for unit_type and relation_type with fail-loud errors on unknown kinds.
Seeds mechanical relation types: produced_by/produces, precedes/succeeds, contains/is_contained_by, references/referenced_by.
Uses deterministic md5-based ids for links keyed by from|edge_type|to.
Keeps the legacy vec_exchanges FLOAT[384] virtual table but demotes it from the live write path.
standouts: Per-lens vector index abstraction with dtype-aware binding and active-axis resolution.
Registry-driven unit and relation type vocabularies enforced at write time.
Fail-loud lineage assertions for atoms/units and gated candidate staging.
Additive migration strategy that leaves exchanges/tool_calls untouched while building the recollection model alongside.
conventions: Idempotent schema operations: CREATE IF NOT EXISTS, INSERT OR IGNORE/REPLACE, and conditional ALTER ADD COLUMN.
SQL identifiers are double-quoted to preserve lens names containing hyphens.
Deterministic primary keys derived from content (md5 for links) rather than random ids.
Heavy inline design-rationale comments citing guide sections and landmine codes.
Default parameters pull the active lens from getLens() so callers rarely pass the spec explicitly.
TypeScript interfaces mirror intended portable/Company field shapes.
concerns: File is large and mixes migrations, schema creation, registry management, vector binding, graph edges, and utility queries, increasing cohesion risk.
Several long comments describe behavior not verifiable from this file alone (e.g., verified against sqlite-vec 0.1.9, task wins).
insertExchange is truncated, so its full transaction scope and fingerprint write path are not visible here.
The legacy vec_exchanges table remains but is no longer written by the live path; downstream readers may still query stale 384-dim vectors.
Candidates table keeps lineage columns nullable, deferring fail-loud checks to commit time, which relies on callers to enforce.
notes: This file is the schema-and-registry backbone of the recollection system: it keeps the old episodic store running while growing a typed, multi-lens, lineage-strict memory graph next to it.
questions: What is the exact implementation of insertExchange beyond the truncation point, especially how it writes the fingerprint and vec row atomically?
How are atoms populated, and what code calls assertAtomLineage?
What does getLens/lensVecSpec in embeddings.js return, and how is the active lens selected?
How are candidates promoted from staged to committed units, and which code enforces the gated ratification step?
Are verdicts written by a separate evaluator, and what verdict_type values are expected?
Does any caller still read vec_exchanges, or is it fully deprecated?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is explicitly additive, builds registry tables and per-lens vector indexes, and leaves exchanges/tool_calls intact while adding the recollection model.
apparent_themes: Additive schema evolution without breaking legacy tables.
Per-lens embedding axes with explicit dtype/dim/metric contracts.
Separation of canonical records (units/fingerprints) from ANN indexes (vec_<space>).
intention_signals: The file appears to be refitting an episodic conversation store into a more general memory substrate that can later be absorbed by a larger system, using data-driven registries, multi-space embeddings, and strict provenance to make backend re-pointing a schema migration rather than a rewrite.
novelty: A dynamic per-lens sqlite-vec table factory with dtype-specific binding (including int8 rounding quantization), paired with portable fingerprint rows and registry tables for both unit kinds and relation kinds, all layered additively over an existing exchanges schema.
contribution: It contributes a migration-safe SQLite foundation, a per-lens vector-index mechanism, typed-unit and relation-type registries, lineage assertions, deterministic link ids, and a clear separation between portable canonical records and ANN indexes.
summary_for_embedding: SQLite schema initializer and data-model helper module for a recollection system, managing exchanges/tool_calls migrations, per-lens sqlite-vec indexes, unit/relation registries, lineage checks, and deterministic provenance links.
intention_for_embedding: To evolve an episodic conversation database into a portable, multi-lens memory graph while preserving legacy tables and enforcing lineage and registry contracts.

symbols.does: Adds missing columns to the exchanges table and triggers dependent migrations.
symbols.description: Iterates over a hardcoded list of ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN statements, checking pragma_table_info to skip existing columns, then calls migrateToolCallsCascade and migrateRecollectionSchema.

symbols.does: Error class thrown when required lineage fields are missing.
symbols.description: Used by assertAtomLineage and assertUnitLineage to reject writes that lack mandatory session_id or project, implementing the fail-loud lineage policy.

symbols.does: Descriptor for a per-lens sqlite-vec0 index.
symbols.description: Carries space name, vector dimension, element type (float/int8/bit), and distance metric; consumed by ensureLensIndex to create a vec_<space> virtual table.

symbols.does: Creates a dtype-aware vec0 virtual table for a given lens spec.
symbols.description: Validates the lens name and dimension, then executes CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vec_<space> USING vec0 with the declared element type, dimension, and metric; returns the double-quoted table identifier.

symbols.does: Resolves the currently active lens into a table name and spec.
symbols.description: Reads the default lens via getLens(), computes its vec spec with lensVecSpec(), and returns the lens entry, the double-quoted vec_<space> identifier, and the spec object.

symbols.does: Materializes the active lens's vec table.
symbols.description: Thin wrapper around ensureLensIndex using getActiveAxis; called during initDatabase so the live ANN table exists before any writes or queries.

symbols.does: Quantizes a float vector to Int8Array using round-to-nearest.
symbols.description: Applies Math.round to each component to recover intended integers from noisy float representations, avoiding truncation bias; used for both vec0 rows and portable fingerprint bytes.

symbols.does: Produces the SQL expression and buffer for binding a vector to a vec0 column.
symbols.description: Checks vector length against the spec dimension; returns vec_int8(?) with a rounded Int8Array buffer for int8 axes, or a plain Float32 buffer for float axes, so INSERT and MATCH use the same dtype contract.

symbols.does: Creates the additive recollection tables if they do not exist.
symbols.description: Defines unit_type, atoms, units, relation_type, links, fingerprints, verdicts, and candidates tables plus supporting indexes, and seeds mechanical relation types; leaves exchanges/tool_calls untouched.

symbols.does: SQLite TEXT column type used throughout schema definitions.
symbols.description: Appears in CREATE TABLE statements for string-valued columns such as ids, addresses, types, and content; not a TypeScript symbol but part of the SQL vocabulary in this file.

symbols.does: Validates that an atom has required session and project lineage.
symbols.description: Throws LineageError if sessionId or project is missing; intended to be called before inserting a raw event into the atoms table.

symbols.does: Validates unit lineage, optionally requiring a round id.
symbols.description: Throws LineageError if sessionId or project is missing, and also when requireRound is true and roundId is absent, distinguishing distilled units from captured atomic units.

symbols.does: Error class for unknown or invalid unit types.
symbols.description: Thrown by unit_type registry helpers when a name is malformed or not present in the registry, enforcing registry-is-truth for unit kinds.

symbols.does: TypeScript interface representing a unit_type registry row.
symbols.description: Declares name, scale, optional extraction prompt reference, embedding lens list, gated flag, and description; mirrors the intended portable shape of unit kind definitions.

symbols.does: Inserts a unit kind into the unit_type registry.
symbols.description: Validates the name and required scale, then performs INSERT OR IGNORE with the current timestamp; adding a new kind is a row operation, not a code change.

symbols.does: Looks up a unit_type row by name, failing loud if unknown.
symbols.description: Queries the registry and returns a UnitTypeRow; if the name is absent, throws UnitTypeError listing known kinds so callers cannot silently use unregistered kinds.

symbols.does: Returns all registered unit types sorted by name.
symbols.description: Queries the unit_type table and maps rows into UnitTypeRow objects, including JSON parsing of embed_lenses.

symbols.does: Writes or replaces a typed unit in the units table.
symbols.description: Resolves the unit_type against the registry, asserts lineage with requireRound=false, and executes INSERT OR REPLACE using the registry scale as a default; canonical record only, not the vector.

symbols.does: Stages a gated record in the candidates table.
symbols.description: Resolves the candidate_type, rejects non-gated kinds, and performs INSERT OR REPLACE with status defaulting to staged; intended as a pre-commit ratification lane for principles and similar gated types.

symbols.does: Error class for unknown or invalid relation types.
symbols.description: Thrown by relation_type registry helpers when an edge kind id is malformed, missing an inverse for directed kinds, or not present in the registry.

symbols.does: TypeScript interface representing a relation_type registry row.
symbols.description: Declares id, directed flag, inverse, near/far projection spaces, label, and description; mirrors the Company's relation_types file shape for later absorption.

symbols.does: Inserts an edge kind into the relation_type registry.
symbols.description: Validates the id and enforces the G11.2 floor by requiring an inverse for directed kinds, then performs INSERT OR IGNORE; new edge kinds are added as rows.

symbols.does: Looks up a relation_type row by id, failing loud if unknown.
symbols.description: Queries the registry and returns a RelationTypeRow; if the id is absent, throws RelationTypeError listing known edge kinds.

symbols.does: Returns all registered relation types sorted by id.
symbols.description: Queries the relation_type table and maps rows into RelationTypeRow objects, converting the directed integer to a boolean.

symbols.does: Idempotently seeds the mechanical crossings vocabulary.
symbols.description: Registers eight directed relation pairs (produced_by/produces, precedes/succeeds, contains/is_contained_by, references/referenced_by) through registerRelationType so the graph has a certain, free skeleton.

symbols.does: Builds a stable URI for a file node.
symbols.description: Returns file://<abs-path>, used as a node identifier in the crossings graph for artefacts.

symbols.does: Builds a stable URI for a session node.
symbols.description: Returns session://<sessionId>, used as a node identifier in the crossings graph.

symbols.does: Builds a stable URI for a project node.
symbols.description: Returns project://<project>, used as a node identifier in the crossings graph.

symbols.does: Inserts one directed crossing edge into the links table.
symbols.description: Resolves edge_type against the registry, stamps direction and inverse from the registry row, computes a deterministic md5 id from from|edge_type|to, and performs INSERT OR IGNORE; returns whether a new row was written.

symbols.does: Rebuilds tool_calls with ON DELETE CASCADE.
symbols.description: Inspects sqlite_master.sql for the legacy schema, removes orphaned rows, recreates the table with a cascading foreign key, copies surviving rows, and re-enables foreign key checks.

symbols.does: Initializes the SQLite database and all schema objects.
symbols.description: Ensures the directory exists, opens the database, loads sqlite-vec, enables WAL, creates exchanges/tool_calls/vec_exchanges, materializes the active axis index, runs migrations, and creates secondary indexes.

symbols.does: Computes a canonical address for an exchange.
symbols.description: Returns exchange://<session-or-exchange-id>/<lineStart>, providing a re-embed-stable identity used as the spine across semantic axes.

symbols.does: Writes an exchange and its embedding to the live per-axis store.
symbols.description: Truncated in this view; intended to insert or replace the exchanges row, write the vector into the active vec_<space> table via vecBind, and record a portable fingerprint row under the provenance address.

symbols.does: Returns id and archivePath for every exchange.
symbols.description: Simple query used by reindexing or repair callers that need to locate archived conversation files.

symbols.does: Returns the latest last_indexed timestamp for an archive path.
symbols.description: Queries exchanges by archive_path and aggregates MAX(last_indexed), returning null when no rows exist.

symbols.does: Removes an exchange by id.
symbols.description: Deletes the exchanges row; because tool_calls has ON DELETE CASCADE, dependent tool call rows are removed automatically.
